# Compute Spot Index

URL: https://chiragmirani.github.io/compute-spot-index/

Compute Spot Index is a public AI compute price tracker. It summarizes public Vast.ai rental offers into median prices per graphics processor-hour, then translates those prices into rough annual rental revenue, annual surplus after total annualized data center costs, and estimated present value for a 100,000-processor AI data center.

The headline valuation uses B300 prices when available, and otherwise falls back to B200.

Primary data:
- Public Vast.ai rental offers, normalized to price per graphics processor-hour.
- Saved public snapshot: https://chiragmirani.github.io/compute-spot-index/data/latest-public.json

Core formula:
- Annual rental revenue = median hourly rental price * 100,000 graphics processors * 8,760 hours * 65% utilization.
- Annual surplus after total annualized costs = annual rental revenue - scaled total annualized data center costs.
- Scaled total annualized costs use Epoch AI's one-gigawatt annual cost model. The one-gigawatt total is about $8.5 billion per year; scaled to a 120-megawatt proxy site, this is about $1.0 billion per year.
- Estimated present value of surplus uses 10 years and a 10% to 15% discount rate.

Reference context:
- Epoch AI, "Frontier Data Centers". Published online at epoch.ai. Retrieved from "https://epoch.ai/data/data-centers" [online resource].
- Amelia Michael and Ben Cottier (2026), "Servers account for 60% of the total cost of ownership of a one-gigawatt AI data center". Published online at epoch.ai. Retrieved from "https://epoch.ai/data-insights/ai-datacenter-cost-breakdown" [online resource]. Accessed 20 May 2026.

Caveat:
- These are public spot-market prices, not hyperscaler procurement costs or net operating margins. The estimates use a stylized annualized cost stack rather than site-specific contracts and still exclude downtime, hardware replacement timing beyond Epoch's stylized lifespans, and the risk that rental prices fall as supply expands.
